title: Extract TUI Tree Building Module
description: Move tree row structures, row collection, tree building, and navigation helpers into `src/agent_takt/tui/tree.py`, depending only on the extracted state layer where required.
acceptance_criteria: `TreeRow`, `build_tree_rows()`, `collect_tree_rows()`, and navigation helpers are relocated to `src/agent_takt/tui/tree.py`.
The tree module depends only on allowed upstream modules and does not introduce cycles.
Call sites continue to resolve the moved symbols without behavior changes.

handoff_summary.completed: Created src/agent_takt/tui/tree.py with all tree row structures, row collection, tree building, and navigation helpers. Updated state.py to import them from tree.py, removing duplicate definitions. Restored Iterable to state.py's typing imports (still needed for summarize_status_counts etc.). Verified no circular imports and all symbols resolve correctly from agent_takt.tui.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The re-export pattern (state.py imports from tree.py and re-exposes symbols) preserves all call sites in __init__.py without modification.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify existing TUI tests pass and that tree module symbols are importable from both agent_takt.tui and agent_takt.tui.tree.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No file-scope conflicts with sibling beads. tree.py is a new file with no prior claims. state.py edits are contained to the import block and removed symbol definitions.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used a re-export pattern: tree.py owns the symbols, state.py does `from .tree import (...)` to re-expose them. This means __init__.py's `from .state import (...)` still resolves without any changes — zero call-site breakage. Alternative was to update __init__.py to import directly from tree.py, but that would require touching a large import block with no benefit. The re-export approach is simpler and reversible. FILTER_STATUS_SETS was recreated in tree.py by inlining the BEAD_* constants directly rather than referencing STATUS_DISPLAY_ORDER (which stays in state.py), avoiding any dependency from tree back to state.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `from agent_takt.tui.tree import TreeRow, build_tree_rows, collect_tree_rows, resolve_selected_index, resolve_selected_bead` all work; (2) `from agent_takt.tui import TreeRow, build_tree_rows, ...` still works unchanged; (3) TUI test suite passes without modification (especially test_tui.py which imports these symbols); (4) No circular import at module load time; (5) FILTER_STATUS_SETS in tree.py produces the same sets as the original in state.py (the FILTER_ALL set now inlines the BEAD_* constants instead of referencing STATUS_DISPLAY_ORDER).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — implementation is complete as specified. The FILTER_ALL set is inlined in tree.py (frozenset of all 6 BEAD_* constants) rather than computed from STATUS_DISPLAY_ORDER; functionally identical since STATUS_DISPLAY_ORDER was exactly those 6 constants.

conflict_risks: No file-scope conflicts with sibling beads. tree.py is a new file with no prior claims. state.py edits are contained to the import block and removed symbol definitions.

execution_history.summary: Extracted tree module from state.py into src/agent_takt/tui/tree.py. Moved TreeRow, build_tree_rows, collect_tree_rows, load_beads, resolve_selected_index, resolve_selected_bead, supported_filter_modes, bead_matches_filter, _status_set, FILTER_DEFAULT/ALL/ACTIONABLE/DEFERRED/DONE, and FILTER_STATUS_SETS. state.py now re-imports all moved symbols from tree.py. No cycles introduced. All call sites in __init__.py continue to resolve without changes.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
